competitors.name: momentum_breakout
competitors.rules: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.

trade_log.reason: fill to cap: breakout > 20d high on volume
fill to cap: breakout > 20d high on volume

competitors.name: blended_momo_rsi
competitors.rules: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.

trade_log.reason: fill to cap: breakout, RSI 64 not overbought
fill to cap: breakout, RSI 66 not overbought

competitors.name: You
competitors.rules: Your real Robinhood account, rebased to the shared origin so it's comparable. Performance only — holdings and trades are deliberately not published.

competitors.name: congress_mirror
competitors.rules: Mirrors members of Congress from the kadoa STOCK Act disclosure mirror: follows every filer with a real track record and buys the in-universe names they disclosed purchasing within 120 days, weighting each name by how many distinct members bought it (consensus = conviction). Traded on the disclosure date — weeks after their actual fill.

competitors.name: mean_reversion
competitors.rules: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.

competitors.name: Robinhood
competitors.rules: Real Robinhood Agentic cash account (••••) running the funded algorithms (100% momentum_breakout) live: each run-robin reruns their signals on the latest bar and trades the real account — buy fresh breakouts, exit on the strategy's sell rule.
competitors.note: Real money — the actual Robinhood Agentic account (••••), not paper. Dollar/share figures are scaled ×100 to the $10,000 display notional like the rest of the board (the live account is ~$100). Allocation: 100% momentum_breakout. Change it in state/robin_alloc.json or just ask.

competitors.name: S&P 500
competitors.rules: Buys SPY with the whole book on the first forward session and holds — the market baseline.

trade_log.reason: buy & hold: all-in on day one

competitors.name: llm_voters
competitors.rules: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.

competitors.name: mirofish_real
competitors.rules: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds, each ranking its best ideas; the panel's rank-weighted consensus (top 5 names, weight ∝ Borda points) rebalanced each tick.

competitors.name: deep_research_analyst
competitors.rules: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).

analyst.framework: Claude for Financial Services — equity-research methodology (/screen, /sector, /comps, /catalysts, /thesis). Data: Robinhood fundamentals + web_search (no paid-vendor MCP connectors).
analyst.action: rotate: exit MU before the 7/10 SKHY listing, trim extended V and TSM, add valuation-floor healthcare (REGN, GILD), upsize the two anchors (LLY into the live Medicare GLP-1 ramp, BAC into 7/15 earnings)
analyst.sizing: 53% deployed across 6 names (LLY 12, BAC 12, TSM 8, V 8, REGN 8, GILD 5), 47% cash while the momentum unwind resolves

regime.label: momentum unwind inside a value/defensive rotation — no-hike relief, but no cuts either
regime.note: June payrolls +57k vs +115k consensus killed the July-hike fear (2yr yields fell) but the Fed's median dot still sees no 2026 cuts (17/18 officials see inflation risk to the upside). Result on 7/2: Dow record close +1.14% (52,900) while SMH fell 4.5% in a second straight day of the semi unwind — memory led (Samsung -7%, SK Hynix -9%). Healthcare and financials are absorbing the rotation: V, UNH, GE, CRWD, MRNA, VRTX all printed 52-wk highs this session while SPY closed flat (-0.13%). This is rotation, not risk-off — but the crowded-momentum side of the tape is still breaking.

analyst.thesis: What changed since 7/1: the unwind I got caught in kept going (SMH -4.5%, second straight down day, memory-led), and my one remaining crowded-memory position now has a hard dated threat — SK Hynix's record $29.4B Nasdaq ADR lists 7/10 at 6.6x forward earnings vs MU's >10x, directly attacking the US-accessibility premium MU has traded on for 13 years. So I exit MU entirely BEFORE the listing (5 tick-days out) rather than after — anticipation, not confirmation. The freed capital goes where the rotation is bid but NOT crowded: REGN at 15.2x (still -19.5% off its high, RSI 76) and GILD at 17.1x with a 2.6% yield (RSI 62) give the healthcare sleeve valuation floors, while the extended 52-wk-high names the tape is chasing (MRNA RSI 86, VRTX RSI 90) are deliberately excluded — buying those today is the exact yesterday's-leadership chase I committed to stop. The two anchors get upsized: LLY 12% into the Medicare GLP-1 Bridge that went LIVE 7/1 ($50/mo copay, ~14M eligible — July script data will show the ramp before the ~Aug 6 print), and BAC 12% into its ~7/15 Q2 report (9 ticks) where a quarter of record rotation volumes and a steepening no-hike curve set up trading/NII beats at 14.5x earnings. TSM stays but trimmed to 8% into its confirmed 7/16 conference — foundry economics are not memory economics, and it held -0.5% over 20 days while the memory basket lost 10-30%. V trims to 8%: the thesis (payments volumes, bank-earnings tailwind) is intact but it printed its 52-wk high today at RSI 86, and I don't add to charts like that anymore.

evidence.point: June nonfarm payrolls rose +57,000 vs +115,000 Dow Jones consensus (May was +129,000); unemployment 4.2% — 'this morning's data makes it difficult to envision a path toward a July Fed hike' (Ian Lyngen, BMO)

7/2 session: Dow +594.83 points (+1.14%) to a record 52,900.07 close while the SMH semiconductor ETF dropped 4.5% — second straight day of semi selling, led by Teradyne -13.6% and KLA -11.5%; Samsung fell 7%+ and SK Hynix 9%+

SK Hynix's amended F-1 (filed 6/30) targets a 7/10 Nasdaq ADR debut under 'SKHY' raising ~$29.4B — the largest ADR listing in history; SK Hynix trades at 6.6x forward P/E vs Micron above 10x, and MU's historical ~35% premium was attributed to 'better access to US investors'

Medicare GLP-1 Bridge went live July 1, 2026: eligible Part D members get Zepbound (KwikPen) and Foundayo for a flat $50 monthly copay through 12/31/2027; ~14 million beneficiaries could qualify; LLY jumped ~5.8% on the program details
JPMorgan confirmed Q2 2026 results for Tuesday July 14 (6:45am ET release); Citi, Wells Fargo, Goldman and Morgan Stanley report the same week, BAC mid-July per its announced 2026 reporting calendar
TSMC's Q2 2026 earnings conference is confirmed for Thursday July 16 at 2:00pm Taiwan time (quiet period 7/6–7/15), releasing Q2 results and Q3 guidance

RH fundamentals 7/2: REGN P/E 15.22 / close 654.24 (-19.5% off its 821.11 52-wk high of 1/9); GILD P/E 17.14, dividend yield 2.56%; vs VRTX 29.5x at a 52-wk high (529.14, RSI 90) and AMGN 25.1x — REGN/GILD are the un-crowded value end of the healthcare bid
RH fundamentals 7/2: BAC P/E 14.48, P/B 1.51, closed 58.69 vs 52-wk high 59.195 (6/25); snapshot shows BAC +12.0% over 20 sessions, RSI 72

Snapshot 20-session moves into 7/2: MU -9.6% (and -19.6% over 5 sessions), MRVL -18.7%, QCOM -29.5%, SOXL -35.5% — vs TSM -0.5% over the same 20 sessions; V printed its 52-wk high (362.13) today at RSI 86

risks: Rotation reverses: if semis bounce hard (an SKHY listing that prices strong could RALLY memory), exiting MU at 975.77 vs my 1119.63 entry locks the -12.8% loss at the low
BAC earnings risk cuts both ways: weak jobs that helped kill the hike also flags credit-cycle softening — a reserve build headline could swamp a trading beat
LLY is +12.3% over 20 sessions; a Medicare Bridge ramp slower than the headline 14M-eligible number implies would deflate the multiple (42.9x trailing)
TSM sits inside the semi-contagion blast radius until 7/16 — foundry/memory distinction can stop mattering in a liquidation
47% cash drags if the Dow-record breadth broadens into a general melt-up
Healthcare sleeve totals 25% (LLY+REGN+GILD) — a sector-wide policy headline (drug pricing) hits all three at once despite their different drivers

data_examined.label: Full 101-name universe screened in randomized order: 1d/5d/20d/60d returns, RSI(14), distance to 20d high and 52-wk high, from the refreshed snapshot (all names carry the settled 7/2 session bar with consolidated volume)
Robinhood fundamentals on all 101 names (P/E, P/B, 52-wk range, dividend, avg volume), market_date 2026-07-02
Macro/regime: June payrolls, Fed dots and July-meeting odds, 7/2 session recap
Catalyst dating: JPM 7/14 (company IR), TSMC 7/16 (company IR), SKHY ADR 7/10 (CNBC/SEC F-1), Medicare GLP-1 Bridge live 7/1 (CMS program via QuiverQuant/PRNewswire)
Prior-tick memory: holdings marks, realized P&L, alpha vs SPY (-11.9pts all-time), Sharpe -5.31, prior reflection

rationale.LLY: Anchor, upsized 10→12%. The dated event is live, not pending: the Medicare GLP-1 Bridge switched on 7/1 — $50/mo flat copay for Zepbound-KwikPen and Foundayo through 2027, ~14M Part D members potentially eligible [DATA: PRNewswire/QuiverQuant]. Consensus models a gradual uptake; a $50 price point against list prices in the hundreds is the kind of demand shock that shows up in weekly July script data, well before the ~Aug 6 Q2 print [ASSUMED: ramp speed is my judgment call]. Chart is strong but not crowded (RSI 60, -1.5% off its high, +12.3%/20d) [DATA: snapshot]. 42.9x trailing is the risk; the ramp is the offset. Buy-limit 1223.50 (~1% over the 7/2 close) so a gap-up Monday doesn't fill me above what the thesis priced.
rationale.BAC: Anchor, upsized 10→12%. Q2 report lands ~7/15, 9 tick-days out (JPM confirmed 7/14; BAC follows per its announced mid-July calendar [DATA: company IR, exact BAC day ASSUMED]). The quarter being reported contained the semi unwind, record Dow prints and violent sector rotation — that's a trading-revenue quarter, and the no-hike payrolls print steepens the deposit-cost picture into the release [ASSUMED: beat direction is my call]. At 14.48x earnings and 1.51x book [DATA: RH fundamentals] there's a valuation floor under the event bet, and the name held +12%/20d with RSI 72 — bid, not parabolic. Was my best holding through the unwind (+1.4% on entry). Buy-limit 59.28.
rationale.TSM: Hold, trimmed 10→8%. The 7/16 investor conference is confirmed (Q2 results + Q3 guidance, quiet period starts 7/6) [DATA: TSMC IR]. Foundry economics are not memory economics: while MU/MRVL/QCOM lost 10-30% over 20 sessions, TSM lost 0.5% [DATA: snapshot] — the AI-capex demand it prices is not the crowding that's unwinding. But it lives in the same blast radius (down -2.2% today, ASML -4.0%), so the position shrinks rather than grows into the print. No limit — the trim leg is a sell, market-on-open.
rationale.V: Hold, trimmed 10→8%. The thesis that put it on (payments volume resilience, bank-earnings-week tailwind, fiscal Q3 print late July) is intact — but V printed its 52-wk high today (362.13) at RSI 86 [DATA: RH fundamentals + snapshot], and two ticks ago I wrote the rule: don't add to yesterday's leadership. 8% keeps the exposure without pretending the entry is fresh. Sell leg market-on-open.
rationale.REGN: New 8%. The un-crowded way to own the healthcare rotation: 15.22x trailing earnings — vs VRTX 29.5x (RSI 90, at its high) and AMGN 25.1x — while still -19.5% below its January high [DATA: RH fundamentals]. It moved +4.7% with the sector bid today from RSI 76, so the rotation has found it but hasn't finished re-rating it [ASSUMED: re-rate continues]. Q2 print ~Aug 4 (~23 ticks) with Dupixent momentum vs the priced-in Eylea biosimilar erosion — a low bar at this multiple. Buy-limit 660.80: if Monday gaps it away, I skip the chase.
rationale.GILD: New 5%. The defensive end of the sleeve: 17.14x, 2.56% dividend, RSI 62, -15.8% off its high [DATA: RH fundamentals] — a valuation-floor holding for a tape where the Fed isn't cutting and momentum keeps breaking. Lenacapavir's launch year and an ~Aug 7 Q2 print are the forward events [ASSUMED: date from GILD's usual calendar]. Sized smallest because it's the least event-dated of the six. Buy-limit 132.55.
rationale.MU: EXIT (was 8%). Two dated reasons to be out this week, not after: (1) SKHY lists on Nasdaq 7/10 — 5 tick-days — at 6.6x forward vs MU's >10x, and the ~35% premium MU has carried for 13 years is explicitly attributed to the US-listing accessibility SKHY now replicates [DATA: CNBC/indmoney]; (2) memory LED today's second leg down (Samsung -7%, SK Hynix -9%, MU -5.5% today and -19.6% over 5 sessions) [DATA: CNBC + snapshot]. The FQ2 $18.7B guide that justified holding half was a fundamentals argument; this is a flows-and-relative-value event that fundamentals don't defend against. Realizes roughly -13% vs my 1119.63 entry — the alternative is holding into the largest ADR listing ever aimed at my position's premium. Sell market-on-open.

reflection.looking_back: Last tick I cut the crowding I'd bought at the top: queued exits of MRVL (entered 7/1 at ~282.62, the same session the basket cracked) and half of MU, holding V/BAC/LLY/TSM/MU at 10/10/10/10/8. The tape then delivered a second unwind day — MRVL -9.9% and MU -5.5% on 7/2 — so the exits queue-filled into more weakness, and the book sits -12.5% all-time vs SPY -0.6%: alpha -11.9pts, Sharpe -5.31 over 14 sessions. Last tick alone was -2.4pts of alpha, driven almost entirely by the melting semi legs.
worked: Cutting MRVL at 101.8x with no catalyst until late August was right and a day early rather than a day late — it fell another -9.9% on 7/2 before the queued exit could even fill [DATA: snapshot 7/2]

BAC (+1.4% vs entry) and LLY (-0.9% on the mark but +5.8% intraday pop on the Medicare Bridge news) were the two holds whose theses strengthened — both carry dated mid-July/live catalysts, exactly the filter I said I'd apply [DATA: analyst_memory + QuiverQuant]
The ~52% cash buffer did its job: a -2.6% book day during a -4.5% SMH session is the position sizing working, not the picks [DATA: analyst_memory]
missed: Halving MU instead of exiting it was half a decision: I cited the SK Hynix listing myself on 7/1 as a ~7/10 risk, then kept 8% anyway — the position lost another -5.5% on 7/2 and I'm now exiting 3 days closer to the event at a worse price [DATA: snapshot]

TSM 'held up better' was true (-2.2% on 7/2 vs MU -5.5%) but I sized it like conviction (10%) when it was really a hold — the foundry/memory distinction protected relative, not absolute, P&L [DATA: snapshot]

I graded the 6/30 disaster as crowding but kept measuring wins in raw P&L: alpha went from -9.7 to -11.9pts in two ticks — the book needs positions that can BEAT SPY, not just names that fall slower than SMH [DATA: analyst_memory]
reflection.adjustment: Finish decisions instead of halving them: when a hold's thesis is invalidated by a dated event (MU vs the 7/10 SKHY listing), exit fully before the event rather than keeping a souvenir position. This tick that means MU to zero, and the freed capital only goes to names carrying BOTH a valuation floor AND a dated catalyst (REGN 15.2x/Aug print, GILD 17.1x/yield, BAC 14.5x/7-15 print) — no entries above RSI 80 no matter how good the story.

swarm.note: Live swarm — 150 fish, each a unique trader profile shown its own random ~20-name slice of the watchlist (with recent headlines), voted via OpenRouter. Independent-voter election; dissenters (ringed) chose CASH.

live.note: Connected to the Agentic cash account •••• — currently unfunded ($0). Deposit into this specific account to trade the real $100. No agent orders placed yet.

rules.momentum_breakout: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.
rules.mean_reversion: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.
rules.blended_momo_rsi: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.
rules.deep_research_analyst: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).
rules.llm_voters: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.
rules.mirofish_real: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds, each ranking its best ideas; the panel's rank-weighted consensus (top 5 names, weight ∝ Borda points) rebalanced each tick.
rules.congress_mirror: Mirrors members of Congress from the kadoa STOCK Act disclosure mirror: follows every filer with a real track record and buys the in-universe names they disclosed purchasing within 120 days, weighting each name by how many distinct members bought it (consensus = conviction). Traded on the disclosure date — weeks after their actual fill.

sources.name: Standings, curves, decision trail (LIVE)
sources.detail: The forward paper test — every competitor trades a $100 book the same way, advanced one trading day per tick (rule strategies via tick.py / bot/engine.py; agents via run_agents.py / bot/paper.py), 5 bps slippage, −15% stop. Young — it grows one session per tick.

sources.name: Backtest reference
sources.detail: A separate Dec–Jun walk-forward backtest (2026-01-02–2026-07-02, in-sample, mostly-bull) is shown inside each rule strategy's detail for context only — it is NOT the live board.

sources.name: Research analyst
sources.detail: Agent-driven on the Claude Code plan: web_search + Robinhood data → one report (state/analyst.json) with target weights per tick. Sources link from each evidence row.

sources.name: Swarm vote
sources.detail: Independent-voter election: 150 unique-profile cheap models (DeepSeek/Gemini/Llama/Haiku via OpenRouter) each read one shared briefing and return a single ballot; tallied in bot/swarm.py. No interaction between fish.

sources.name: Real-MiroFish swarm
sources.detail: Social-simulation swarm (bot/mirofish.py): persona agents with memory interact over multiple rounds — each re-ranks its best ideas seeing its own prior view and its neighbours' latest — so a consensus forms via peer influence (the opposite of the independent swarm). The panel's rank-weighted (Borda) consensus becomes a multi-name book (top 5 names, weight ∝ points). Tiered cost (cheap/default/qwen); seeded from the briefing + optional world-events news.

sources.name: Live account
sources.detail: Robinhood MCP, Agentic cash account ••••, via get_portfolio / get_equity_positions / get_equity_orders.

sources.name: Display scale
sources.detail: The real paper books are $100 each; every dollar and share figure on this site is shown ×100 (a $10,000 book) so holdings read in whole shares and dollars. Per-share prices and percentages are unscaled.
